# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: logagentdeploy
spec:
  replicas: 3 # number of pods you want in your deployment, adjust as needed for production use case.  
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: myapp-logagentset
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: myapp-logagentset
    spec:
      containers: # list of pod's container definitions, adjust as needed for production use case.  
       - name: logagentcontainer1    
         image: yourimagename/logging_image 
         volumeMounts: # mount host directories to the logging agent in each Pod instance   
           - name: varlog            
             mountPath: /var/log             
           - name: vardata           
             mountPath: /var/lib/docker  
      securityContext:    
        runAsUser: 1000          # change this to the user ID that will own these volumes and containers.
